title: Treeline crest and sheltered descent phase
summary: Fifty-eighth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave gives treeline a stronger crest and a calmer sheltered-descent identity so its vertical family feels more complete.

execution_order.why_now: Treeline is ready to feel more like a full vertical family rather than just a lift.
A stronger crest can make the treeline climb feel more memorable without broadening it.
The crest must stay chip-safe, readable, and calm.
A memorable return is the next gain after the crest itself.
A calmer return can complete the treeline family without new HUD or cue language.
The return should feel fair, not harsher.

guardrails: Do not add a harsher second climb family.
Reuse current hint, recovery, and shelter language.
Preserve chip-safe headroom and browser readability.
acceptance_focus: Treeline has a fuller start-to-return vertical identity.
The crest feels memorable but calm.
The descent stays recoverable and readable.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-treeline-crest-and-sheltered-descent-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's next long-push wave after the current treeline/tundra vertical work: give treeline a stronger crest and a sheltered descent without adding a harsher climb family or a new cue language.
